title: galeobdolon caucasicum
intro: Galeobdolon caucasicum, also known as Caucasus bugle or bugle, is a perennial plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). It has been traditionally used in medicine for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing inflammation, and acting as an antispasmodic. In herbal preparations, Galeobdolon caucasicum is typically used in infusions, decoctions, or tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
